item.name: Folders

description.content: Manage Google Cloud folders. Folders provide an additional grouping mechanism and isolation boundary between projects. They can be used to model organizational structure, departments, teams, or environments.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform List Folders

request.name: Google Cloud Platform List Folders

description.content: Lists the folders that are direct descendants of the specified parent resource. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.list permission on the parent. Folders are listed in alphabetical order by display name.

query.description: (Required) The name of the parent resource whose folders are to be listed. Must be of the form folders/{folder_id} or organizations/{org_id}.
Maximum number of folders to return.
Pagination token from a previous list call.
Indicate that folders in the DELETE_REQUESTED state should also be returned.

response.name: Successful response containing a list of folders.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

request.name: Google Cloud Platform Create a Folder

description.content: Creates a folder in the resource hierarchy under the specified parent. The parent can be an organization or another folder. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.create permission on the parent.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation that can be polled for completion.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Folder

request.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Folder

description.content: Requests deletion of a folder. The folder is moved into the DELETE_REQUESTED state and enters a 30-day grace period. During this period, the folder can be restored. After the grace period, the folder is permanently deleted. The folder must be empty before it can be deleted. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.delete permission on the folder.

variable.description: (Required) The resource name of the folder to delete.

response.name: Operation started successfully. Returns a long-running operation.

request.name: Google Cloud Platform Undelete a Folder

description.content: Cancels the deletion request for a folder. This method can only be called within the 30-day grace period. The caller must have the resourcemanager.folders.undelete permission on the folder.

response.name: The folder has been restored from pending deletion.

item.name: Organizations

description.content: Manage Google Cloud organizations. An organization is the root node in the Google Cloud resource hierarchy, typically representing a company or business entity. Organizations are tied to a Google Workspace or Cloud Identity account.

item.name: Projects

description.content: Manage Google Cloud projects. A project is the base-level organizing entity in Google Cloud and is required to use most Google Cloud services. Projects contain resources and are associated with billing accounts.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform List Projects

request.name: Google Cloud Platform List Projects

description.content: Lists projects that are direct children of the specified parent resource. Projects are listed in alphabetical order by display name. The caller must have the resourcemanager.projects.list permission on the parent resource.

query.description: (Required) The name of the parent resource whose projects are to be listed. Must be of the form folders/{folder_id} or organizations/{org_id}.
Maximum number of projects to return in the response. The server can return fewer results. If unspecified, the server picks an appropriate default value.
A pagination token returned from a previous call to ListProjects that indicates where this listing should continue from.
Indicate that projects in the DELETE_REQUESTED state should also be returned.

response.name: Successful response containing a list of projects.

item.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Project

request.name: Google Cloud Platform Delete a Project

description.content: Marks the project identified by the specified name to be deleted. This method sets the project lifecycle state to DELETE_REQUESTED. The project is not immediately deleted but enters a 30-day grace period during which it can be restored using undelete. After 30 days, the project and all its resources are permanently deleted.

variable.description: (Required) The name of the project to delete.

item.name: TagBindings

description.content: Manage tag bindings that associate tag values with Google Cloud resources. Tag bindings connect tag values to specific resources in the hierarchy.

query.description: (Required) The full resource name of the resource whose bindings are being listed. Example: //cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com/projects/123.

info.name: Google Cloud Platform Google Cloud Resource Manager API

description.content:
Programmatically manage Google Cloud projects, folders, and organizations. The Cloud Resource Manager API enables you to create, read, update, and delete projects, folders, and organizations in the Google Cloud resource hierarchy. It provides the foundation for organizing and governing Google Cloud resources through a hierarchical structure of organizations, folders, and projects.

Contact Support:
 Name: Google Cloud Platform
